quality.rationale: 超原理解释清晰+粒子环物理模型+公式推导完整

origin.question: Problem 5.1. Calculate the nonzero components in an inertial frame S of the stress-energy tensor for the following systems:(c) Two such rings of particles, one rotating clockwise, the other counter-clockwise, at the same radius a. The particles do not collide or interact with each other in any way.
origin.solution: If we have an additional ring rotating in the opposite direction then that ring's stress-energy is the same as that in (b), with the sign of $\beta = \omega a$ reversed. We can then add the two cases. In the sum, terms linear in $\beta$ disappear; terms quadratic in $\beta$ are doubled. In polar coordinates, the nonzero components are$$ \mathbf {T}^{0 0} = \mathbf {T}^{\hat {\phi} \hat {\phi}} / \beta^{2} = \gamma \mathrm {N m} \delta (\mathrm {r} - \mathrm {a}) \delta (\mathrm {z}) / \pi \mathrm {a}.$$
